executive_summary: Global systemic risk remains moderate as a sharp energy spike in the Middle East compounds existing pressure on global logistics networks. Disproportionate media escalation regarding Taiwan highlights an ongoing information threat that currently lacks corresponding physical confirmation. Speculative technology markets present elevated exposure but are temporarily buffered by highly stable global fuel reserves.
correlations: The pronounced Middle East energy spike directly drives the elevated supply chain stress score through increased maritime transit risks and projected fuel distribution costs.
